sets.q: A machine does 1 200 J of work in 4 seconds. What is its power output?
A worker does 900 J of work in 3 seconds. What is the power?
An engine does 6 000 J of work in 12 seconds. What is its power output?
A student does 450 J of work in 9 seconds. What is their power output?
A pump does 8 000 J of work in 20 seconds. What is its power?
A 70 kg person climbs 3 m of stairs in 6 seconds. What is their power output? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given:
Work done against gravity: W = mgh
Then P = W / t

sets.q: A 50 kg student walks up 4 m of stairs in 8 seconds. What is their power? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 60 kg athlete climbs 5 m in 10 seconds. What is their power output? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 40 kg child runs up a 6 m staircase in 12 seconds. What is their average power? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 80 kg man walks up 2 m of stairs in 4 seconds. What is his power output? (g = 10 m/s²)
What is the unit of power in the SI system?
One watt (1 W) is equivalent to:
A device rated at 1 000 W consumes how many joules per second?
sets.given: P = W / t → W = P × t. 1 second: W = 1 000 × 1

sets.q: The SI unit of power is named after which scientist?
sets.given: The watt is a unit honoring a Scottish inventor and engineer.

sets.q: Which of the following is equivalent to 1 kilowatt (1 kW)?
A vehicle travels at a constant speed of 15 m/s with a driving force of 800 N. What is the engine's power output?
A car moves at 20 m/s with an engine force of 500 N. What is the power?
A cyclist pedals at 6 m/s with a forward force of 120 N. What is their power output?
A boat moves at 4 m/s with an engine force of 2 500 N. What is the power output?
A train moves at 30 m/s with a tractive force of 15 000 N. What is the engine's power?
A 500 W motor runs for 30 seconds. How much work does it do?
A 200 W light bulb is on for 5 minutes. How much energy does it use?
A 1 500 W appliance runs for 8 seconds. How much work is done?
A 300 W pump operates for 2 minutes. How much energy does it deliver?
A 750 W fan runs for 40 seconds. How much work is done?
Two machines A and B do the same amount of work. Machine A takes 5 s; Machine B takes 20 s. Which is more powerful?
sets.given: P = W / t. Same W, different t. More power = less time.
opts: Machine A — it does the same work in less time
Machine B — it takes longer so it works harder
Cannot be determined without knowing the work done

sets.q: Machine X does 1 000 J in 2 s; Machine Y does 1 000 J in 5 s. Which machine is more powerful?
sets.given: P = W / t. Compare power outputs.
opts: Machine Y — it conserves more energy
Depends on the type of machine

sets.q: A slow pump takes 60 s to lift water; a powerful pump takes 10 s. If they move the same amount of water, which is more powerful?
sets.given: Same work done (same water lifted), different time.
opts: The powerful pump — it does the same work in 1/6 the time
The slow pump — it's more efficient

sets.q: Engines A and B both produce 10 000 J of work. A takes 10 s; B takes 50 s. What is the ratio of power A to power B?
Two students carry identical loads up the same flight of stairs. Student A takes 10 s; Student B takes 15 s. Who generates more power?
sets.given: Same work (same load, same height). P = W / t.
opts: Student A — does the same work in less time
Student B — slower means more sustained effort

sets.q: A machine does 4 500 J of work. If its power output is 150 W, how long does it take?
A 200 W pump does 10 000 J of work. How long does it take?
A 500 W motor does 7 500 J of work. How long does it run?
A 250 W engine does 5 000 J of work. How long does it take?
A 1 000 W machine does 12 000 J of work. How much time does it need?
Convert 3 kW to watts.
A microwave is rated at 1.2 kW. What is this in watts?
An air conditioner is rated at 2.5 kW. Convert to watts.
A generator produces 15 kW. What is this in watts?
A solar panel produces 0.35 kW. What is this in watts?
A 60 kg person runs up a 10 m ramp in 12 seconds. What is their average power? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 45 kg student climbs a 8 m ladder in 9 seconds. What is their average power? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 90 kg worker carries bricks to a height of 6 m in 18 seconds. What is their average power? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 30 kg child runs up a 5 m staircase in 5 seconds. What is the average power output? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 55 kg athlete lifts herself 4 m on gymnastic rings in 2 seconds. What is her power? (g = 10 m/s²)
Engine A: 2 000 W for 10 s. Engine B: 5 000 W for 4 s. Which does more work?
sets.given: W = P × t for each engine.
opts: Both do 20 000 J — they are equal

sets.q: A pump rated at 400 W runs for 30 s. A second pump rated at 300 W runs for 40 s. Which does more work?
sets.given: W = P × t for each pump.

sets.q: Motor X: 600 W for 20 s. Motor Y: 800 W for 15 s. Which does more work?
sets.given: W = P × t for each motor.

sets.q: Fan A: 100 W for 60 s. Fan B: 150 W for 40 s. Which consumes more energy?
sets.given: E = P × t for each fan.

sets.q: A 50 W bulb runs for 2 hours. A 100 W bulb runs for 1 hour. Which uses more electrical energy?
sets.given: E = P × t (convert hours to seconds).
opts: Both use the same energy — 360 000 J
